english_query: why we should not accept something just because many people believe it
ugf_response: is a thought that can feel like a big wind and rain. it feels safe to go with what many think. but the number of people who think something does not make it true. let us look at why this can happen step by step. first, think about how many people think a certain thing is right. When a lot of people say the same, they may only have heard the same story or see the same picture of the world. The more often the story is told, the stronger the feeling that it is right. a later time, many people believed the earth was flat. That flat earth was not true, but the feeling of being right led to it. Second, a belief can be strong even if it is not true. a person can feel sure about an idea without checking the facts that back it up. This feeling can stop a person from looking for new facts. If a person does not check the facts, they may keep a wrong idea for a long time. Third, each of us has a mind that can work in some ways and not in others. Our thoughts are made of many parts that help us act. Some of these parts are good and can cause hurt. When we only listen to what many think, we may miss a fact that the few thinks never help. Also, if we only follow what many think we might ignore signs that show the idea is not right. It is better to use your own mind, ask for facts, try out ideas on their own, and be ready to change a mind if new facts show another view. In short, the number of voices that think something is true is not a safe sign that the thing is true. We must check the idea itself, not just the number of heads that agree.
